briefdescription.para: The Linux Foundation.

description.para: As per Bluetooth Core Specification 6.2, Vol 3, Part C, Appendix A.

briefdescription.para: Recommended minimum scan interval for fast scanning.
Recommended maximum scan interval for fast scanning.
Recommended scan window for fast scanning.
Recommended scan interval for slow scanning (mode 1).
Recommended scan window for slow scanning (mode 1).
Recommended scan interval for slow scanning (mode 2).
Recommended scan window for slow scanning (mode 2).
Recommended minimum advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 1).
Recommended maximum advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 1).
Recommended minimum advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 2).
Recommended maximum advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 2).
Recommended minimum advertising interval for slow advertising.
Recommended maximum advertising interval for slow advertising.
Recommended minimum periodic advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 1).
Recommended maximum periodic advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 1).
Recommended minimum periodic advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 2).
Recommended maximum periodic advertising interval for fast advertising (mode 2).
Recommended minimum periodic advertising interval for slow advertising.
Recommended maximum periodic advertising interval for slow advertising.
Recommended minimum initial connection interval.
Recommended maximum initial connection interval.
Convenience macro for when no PHY is set.
LE 1M PHY.
LE 2M PHY.
LE Coded PHY, coding scheme not specified.
LE Coded S=8 PHY.

detaileddescription.para: Only used for advertising reports when Kconfig BT_EXT_ADV_CODING_SELECTION is enabled.

briefdescription.para: LE Coded S=2 PHY.

detaileddescription.para: Only used for advertising reports when Kconfig BT_EXT_ADV_CODING_SELECTION is enabled.

briefdescription.para: LE PHY types.
Scannable and connectable advertising.
Directed connectable advertising.
Non-connectable and scannable advertising.
Non-connectable and non-scannable advertising.
Additional advertising data requested by an active scanner.
Extended advertising, see advertising properties.
Advertising PDU types.
Connectable advertising.
Scannable advertising.
Directed advertising.
Additional advertising data requested by an active scanner.
Extended advertising.
Advertising PDU properties.
Angle of Arrival.
Angle of Departure with 1 us slots.
Angle of Departure with 2 us slots.
No extensions.
Constant Tone Extension (CTE) types.
Unknown.
251 ppm to 500 ppm
151 ppm to 250 ppm
101 ppm to 150 ppm
76 ppm to 100 ppm
51 ppm to 75 ppm
31 ppm to 50 ppm
21 ppm to 30 ppm
0 ppm to 20 ppm
Peripheral sleep clock accuracy (SCA) in ppm (parts per million)
Maximum advertising data length.
Maximum extended advertising data length.

simplesect.para: The maximum advertising data length that can be sent by an extended advertiser is defined by the controller.

briefdescription.para: Default data length.
Maximum data length.
Default data time.
Maximum data time.
Minimum advertising set number.
Maximum advertising set number.
Maximum number of consecutive periodic advertisement events that can be skipped after a successful receive.
Minimum Periodic Advertising Timeout (N * 10 ms)
Maximum Periodic Advertising Timeout (N * 10 ms)
Minimum Periodic Advertising Interval (N * 1.25 ms)
Maximum Periodic Advertising Interval (N * 1.25 ms)
Convert periodic advertising interval (N * 0.625 ms) to microseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is BT_LE_ADV_INTERVAL_MIN to BT_LE_ADV_INTERVAL_MAX

briefdescription.para: Convert periodic advertising interval (N * 0.625 ms) to milliseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is BT_LE_ADV_INTERVAL_MIN to BT_LE_ADV_INTERVAL_MAX

simplesect.para: When intervals cannot be represented in milliseconds, this will round down. For example BT_GAP_ADV_INTERVAL_TO_MS(0x0021) will become 20 ms instead of 20.625 ms

briefdescription.para: Convert isochronous interval (N * 1.25 ms) to microseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is BT_HCI_ISO_INTERVAL_MIN to BT_HCI_ISO_INTERVAL_MAX

briefdescription.para: Convert isochronous interval (N * 1.25 ms) to milliseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is BT_HCI_ISO_INTERVAL_MIN to BT_HCI_ISO_INTERVAL_MAX

simplesect.para: When intervals cannot be represented in milliseconds, this will round down. For example BT_GAP_ISO_INTERVAL_TO_MS(0x0005) will become 6 ms instead of 6.25 ms

briefdescription.para: Convert periodic advertising interval (N * 1.25 ms) to microseconds *.

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is BT_HCI_LE_PER_ADV_INTERVAL_MIN to BT_HCI_LE_PER_ADV_INTERVAL_MAX

briefdescription.para: Convert periodic advertising interval (N * 1.25 ms) to milliseconds.

simplesect.para: When intervals cannot be represented in milliseconds, this will round down. For example BT_GAP_PER_ADV_INTERVAL_TO_MS(0x0009) will become 11 ms instead of 11.25 ms

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to advertising interval units (0.625 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_ADV_INTERVAL(21000) will become 20625 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to advertising interval units (0.625 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_ADV_INTERVAL(21) will become 20.625 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to periodic advertising interval units (1.25 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_PER_ADV_INTERVAL(11000) will become 10000 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to periodic advertising interval units (1.25 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_PER_ADV_INTERVAL(11) will become 10 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to periodic advertising sync timeout units (10 ms)

simplesect.para: If _timeout is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_PER_ADV_SYNC_TIMEOUT(4005) will become 4000 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to periodic advertising sync timeout units (10 ms)

simplesect.para: If _timeout is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_PER_ADV_SYNC_TIMEOUT(4005000) will become 4000000 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to scan interval units (0.625 ms)

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is 2500 to 40959375 if CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV else 2500 to 10240000

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_SCAN_INTERVAL(21000) will become 20625 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to scan interval units (0.625 ms)

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _interval is 2.5 to 40959.375 if CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV else 2500 to 10240

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_SCAN_INTERVAL(21) will become 20.625 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to scan window units (0.625 ms)

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _window is 2500 to 40959375 if CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV else 2500 to 10240000

simplesect.para: If _window is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_SCAN_WINDOW(21000) will become 20625 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to scan window units (0.625 ms)

detaileddescription.para: Value range of _window is 2.5 to 40959.375 if CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV else 2500 to 10240

simplesect.para: If _window is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_SCAN_WINDOW(21) will become 20.625 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to connection interval units (1.25 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_CONN_INTERVAL(21000) will become 20000 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to connection interval units (1.25 ms)

simplesect.para: If _interval is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_CONN_INTERVAL(21) will become 20 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to connection supervision timeout units (10 ms)

simplesect.para: If _timeout is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_CONN_TIMEOUT(4005) will become 4000 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert microseconds to connection supervision timeout units (10 ms)

simplesect.para: If _timeout is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_CONN_TIMEOUT(4005000) will become 4000000 microseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to connection event length units (0.625)

simplesect.para: If _event_len is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_US_TO_CONN_EVENT_LEN(21000) will become 20625 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Convert milliseconds to connection event length units (0.625)

simplesect.para: If _event_len is not a multiple of the unit, it will round down to nearest. For example BT_GAP_MS_TO_CONN_EVENT_LEN(21) will become 20.625 milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Encode 40 least significant bits of 64-bit LE Supported Features into array values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 40 least significant bits of 64-bit LE Supported Features value into advertising data. The number of bits that are encoded is a number of LE Supported Features defined by BT 5.3 Core specification.
Example of how to encode the 0x000000DFF00DF00D into advertising data.

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for LE Supported Features value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Encode 4 least significant bytes of 64-bit LE Supported Features into 4 bytes long array of values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 64-bit LE Supported Features value into advertising data. The macro encodes 4 least significant bytes into advertising data. Other 4 bytes are not encoded.
Example of how to encode the 0x000000DFF00DF00D into advertising data.

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for LE Supported Features value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Encode 3 least significant bytes of 64-bit LE Supported Features into 3 bytes long array of values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 64-bit LE Supported Features value into advertising data. The macro encodes 3 least significant bytes into advertising data. Other 5 bytes are not encoded.
Example of how to encode the 0x000000DFF00DF00D into advertising data.

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for LE Supported Features value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Encode 2 least significant bytes of 64-bit LE Supported Features into 2 bytes long array of values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 64-bit LE Supported Features value into advertising data. The macro encodes 3 least significant bytes into advertising data. Other 6 bytes are not encoded.
Example of how to encode the 0x000000DFF00DF00D into advertising data.

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for LE Supported Features value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Encode the least significant byte of 64-bit LE Supported Features into single byte long array.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 64-bit LE Supported Features value into advertising data. The macro encodes the least significant byte into advertising data. Other 7 bytes are not encoded.
Example of how to encode the 0x000000DFF00DF00D into advertising data.

simplesect.para: The value of least significant byte of LE Supported Features value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

memberdef.name: BT_LE_SUPP_FEAT_VALIDATE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(!((w64) & (~BIT64_MASK(40))), \ "RFU bit in LE Supported Features are not zeros.")

briefdescription.para: Validate whether LE Supported Features value does not use bits that are reserved for future use.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to check if w64 has zeros as bits 40-63. The macro is compliant with BT 5.3 Core Specification where bits 0-40 has assigned values. In case of invalid value, build time error is reported.

briefdescription.para: Bluetooth Generic Access Profile defines and Assigned Numbers.
